I spent some time digging around to see if Klaviyo allows you to use an existing Twilio number but couldn’t find anything published online (including in their help center). My guess is that the answer will probably be “no”, unfortunately. They don’t have publicly available support, but you should be able to get in touch here. The question for them is whether or not they’ll let you keep ownership of your Twilio number or get access to an API key for the underlying Twilio number so you can use it with another service (Atlas).

Dang, sorry about that. There could be a few motivations for this, but I suppose it’s a moot point at the moment. With Atlas, you can either connect an existing Twilio number that you have access to or you can create a number through Atlas (we automatically provision it under the hood).

Hi Piyush, et al,We're deploying this to prod finally and I realize we're running in to an issue and I wanted to see if you might have some ideas or an API tweak to help.workflow is as initially described: one of our users sends a 'reply' in our app, we need to push that reply to Atlas. If we create a new customer we have the customer ID and can subsequently create the new message. however - if that 'customer' already exists a subsequent create call will fail with a 500.looking at customers endpoints it looks like we need to list all to get the atlas ID of a customer, that's pretty awkward/painful for thousands of customers. I suspect we could also ingest a Atlas webhook for new customers on our side...I was thinking it might be easier if we could lookup an individual atlas customer by our external ID. we can set that going forward in the create customer and then if we get a 500 on the create we'd turn around and lookup the customer via the externalUserId field we'd previously set. effectively "retrieve one customer by externalUserId" and then post the message via the id.I'm sure you some other ideas that might work, very happy to consider any and all options.

Hey Jeremiah,Yes, this is not ideal.Are you using Atlas to identify customers? Reason, the external id solution would work only if the user has already been identified.And, I agree this is the best way to create a conversation, using external_id

in this case, yes, we have a local userID for the user on our side. we've also set that in the atlas frontend JS via window?.Atlas?.identify({ userId: user.id, name: user.display_name, email: user.email })I'm guessing that userId tracks to a customer externalUserId?
yes! userId: user.id, perfectI will update this API to accept externalID
I really appreciate it!
Hey Jeremiah! Have changed the API to accept the external user id as well. Will be shipping it in a few hours. Will let you know once this is in production.

Your customer went offline from chat and since they had email address nooralam.91squarefeet@rdash.io assigned, our system attempted to re-route your message to email which failed because this particular email account does not exist. You could see the error message by hovering over (i) next to delivery status.To disable re-routing attempt, please uncheck Enable fallback channels on Chat Configuration page and press Save at the bottom.Then the following messages will be delivered to chat and visible when the user comes back to your app.

What they typically do is create separate responses and chatbot workflows depending on the language of the user.We don’t have support to do this on an agent basis, yet, but would love to talk through your use case — it’s definitely something we could expedite if needed!

I'm trying to decide where to implement the identify call. What is best practice for that? Do i just need to call it whenever a user signs in?

We store the identity of a user on local storage for 30 days, so if your user session are less than 30 days, you can just call it when user sign in. But if they are longer, you can call it when they load the application -- don't worry about duplicate calls, we have logic in place to dedupe then if duplicate calls are being made.

Sample Code for identify call// App Id is available at app.getatlas.ioconst APP_ID = null;// Token is available at app.getatlas.ioconst SEGMENTTOATLAS_TOKEN = null;const BASE_URL = 'api.getatlas.io';const API_VERSION = 'v1';async function onIdentify(event, settings) { const endpoint = 'app.getatlas.io'; if (!event.userId) {  throw new EventNotSupported('Mandatory parameters are missing'); } if (!APP_ID) {  console.log('Bad Configuration - APP_ID - missing');  return; } atlasEvent = {  appId: APP_ID,  version: 5,  userId: event.userId,  customFields: event.traits.customFields,  fields: event.traits.fields,  email: event.traits.email,  name: event.traits.name,  isVisitor: event.traits.isVisitor || false }; if (event.traits.visitorId) {  atlasEvent['visitorId'] = event.traits.visitorId; } if (event.traits.account) {  atlasEvent['account'] = event.traits.account; } try {  response = await fetch(endpoint, {   method: 'POST',   headers: {    'Content-Type': 'application/json'   },   body: JSON.stringify(atlasEvent)  });  if (response.status >= 500 || response.status === 429) {   throw new RetryError(Failed with ${response.status});  } } catch (error) {  throw new RetryError(error.message); }}

Hi Tir,Apologies for the delay in response.1. We pull the data every 90 mins 2. Right now we don’t have segments filter for contacts/accounts but this is on the roadmap for the start of Q4 — will let know as soon as we go live with this3. There are couple of ways to do this     a. Best option would be filter at source when you are forwarding emails to Atlas— main reason for this is that this way you won’t these tickets in reports or workflow. If this is hard to add or maintain this filter, then you could one of the following     b. Right now we don’t have a way to change the status of tickets in via routing but we are already in process on add this for another customer and should have this in a day or so — you can mark ticket from certain customer as closed as soon as they come up.      c. Also, you can create a custom inboxes and filter out any tickets that you don’t want to from that custom Inbox.
I will let you know 3b get launched in a day or so — let me know if you have any other questions.
hey Tor, option 3b is live now. So for option#3 you can try any of the solutions.

@Jocelyn Weigel I found out what is going on — going to add in a change that you don’t have to do anything and it all works but for next day or so here is the work around:• If you got to customer profile page and don’t see the ticket • go back to inbox, refresh the page• click on ticket again and you will see the ticket (this is the root cause of the problem: A user start chatting using Atlas chat — but Atlas doesn’t know the details user yet — a ticket gets created in atlas as a visitor, after sometime Rootine website calls identify method to let Atlas know who the user is, so Atlas will move tickets/sessions etc for this visitor to actual user. But this change is not yet updated on your inbox so it takes you to old visitor profile. We will make it so that inbox know about this change as soon as possible)

Let me check on our end — but I think we should be able to request private channels as well (as long as their isn’t a limitation on slack API). If that’s possible, we will change that today and let you know.

ticket_subject: New ticket getting created on customer response
message: Hi @Gabriele Inacio, we found the root of the issue.The user that you were talking to here (Jacsantos Assessori) was sending messages from one number (+5511981712104) but Portao3's identify call was resetting his number to the one used in your database (+5511964884965) when he logged in.To resolve this, we’re updating our logic so that your messages will go to the number that started the conversation rather than the number on file for that user.However, to prevent confusion in the future, we’d recommend updating this user’s number in Portao3 so that any new outbound tickets you send to them go to the correct number.

message: @Gabriele Inacio & @Fernando Nery, we’ve seen several cases where:1. customers have a primary number that comes from identify or Hubspot 2. a customer will subsequently send a message from a different number, so they show up as a visitorTo prevent these in the future, if you happen to have a file with your customers with multiple numbers per customer, we can give you a way to upload that so that this doesn’t occur. In the situation where these numbers are numbers you’ve never seen before, we’ll have a Merge Customer flow built in the not-too-distant future to cover these cases.

Regarding AtlasIdentity cookie, we are taking a look and get back to you in a day or so.

Hello Rahul and team, I want to make it possible for our users to be able to report a bug, connected to our Sentry.io monitoring/reporting (https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/javascript/guides/nextjs/user-feedback/). I'm thinking their User Feedback API would do the trick. How can I integrate this with Atlas -- the simplest ways I had in mind are to add a "Report a Bug" link in your chat pop-up menu that would work with their feedback API. Do you know how I can accomplish this?
Hello William!Thanks for pointing this feature out. We will experiment with Sentry API and respond tomorrow morning.

Actually, you can register a callback function to run on a menu item click. 1. The function needs to be registered with the Atlas widget according to this article. 2. Callback name needs to be assigned to a menu option in App Configuration > Chat > Widget menu section. Can you please try these steps and let us know if you need additional help.

ticket_subject: Fail to receive message from Twilio
message: Hey Gabriele, we just checked and found this message still in Twilio’s servers. If message delivery fails, they retry to send with a delay, so it was scheduled to arrive a little later today it looks like. We’re updating this retry timer so that the delay is much shorter if there are any failed messages in the future (these are very rare but can happen from time to time due to lots of different factors in the network)

ticket_subject: Add ticket number to external API
message: hey @kripa shankar Singh Gaharwar So the startedAt and createdAt are usually the same whenever there’s a new ticket coming in. @Jon OBryan Forgot to ask during our demo yesterday - does Atlas has calling and SMS capabilities? If we were to integrate it with HubSpot, are we able to use those capabilities through HubSpot as well? Let me know, thanks!

Hi @Michaela McDermott! You can send SMS natively through us and you can call via integration (eg, Dialpad or Aircall).• For SMS, you just choose the channel as SMS when you’re sending a new message. Anyone who sends an SMS to you will automatically have a ticket created (or, if they have a ticket open, the message will be associated with that ticket). They show up in your timeline like other tickets, • For phone, you will see any calls (in or out) in the customer timeline, and if you have it enabled w/ the telephony tool (Dialpad, Aircall), you can see the call transcript and a link to the call recording. To make a call, you can click on the phone button in the customer’s info pane (from within Atlas).Lmk if any of that is unclear and I can send a Loom or hop on a quick call to clarify.
Makes sense, thank you! Cc @Brandon Hancock for vis

Will circle back then. It would be helpful to see your general roadmap if you have it available to share.

@Michaela McDermott, @Thomas Epting, and @Brandon Hancock, here’s what’s on our general roadmap (major features to not bore you). The normal caveats apply:• the bigger something is and the farther out it is, the less certain the timeline is• we’ll likely do quite a few things not on the roadmap due to customer needs• we’re hiring 2-3 more engineers right now and hope to accelerate this scheduleQ2• Customer portal• Advanced workflow automations• Mobile app• Conditional fields• Outbound messaging (eg, campaigns/broadcast messages to email, Slack, SMS, WhatsApp, and chat)• Improved reporting (download underlying chart data to CSV, adjust for biz hrs, additional charts)• Improved help center (additional template, much richer text editing, improved customer facing UI)• Dark mode• Suggested responses (AI-driven based off of your help center and tickets)• Suggested articles (AI-driven based off of your help center and tickets)• Zeus (AI-driven chatbot)Q3• Phone as a channel• Changelogs (publish your product updates in your app for customers to see)• Improved outbound messaging (eg, sequences & transactional messages)• Surveys• Additional project management integrations (Asana, ClickUp, Github issues)• Additional CRM integrations (Pipedrive, Attio• Stripe integrationQ4• Advanced team management• Role-based permissions• Audit logs• Product insights• Product requests board• Internationalization
